title: ononis alopecuroides var. salzmanniana
intro: Ononis alopecuroides var. salzmanniana, also known as Salzmann's Spanish clover or Ononis salzmanniana, is a perennial plant species within the legume family, Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of menopause. The health benefits of Ononis alopecuroides var. salzmanniana include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve circulation, and alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
